title: mentha litoralis var. interrupta
intro: Mentha litoralis var. interrupta, also known as coastal peppermint or peppermint by the sea, is a variant of the peppermint plant that is native to coastal regions and is characterized by its interrupted leaves. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent. The health benefits of Mentha litoralis var. interrupta include relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Mentha litoralis var. interrupta can be prepared as an herbal tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine to promote relaxation and soothe the body.
